titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Douglas Conrad Eastern and Central European LGBT Interviews and Supporting Materials
Conrad (Douglas) Eastern and Central European LGBT Interviews and Supporting Materials
titlestmt.author: compiled by Mary Caldera

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday March 13, 2020 at 07:51

did.unittitle: Douglas Conrad Eastern and Central European LGBT interviews and supporting materials
did.abstract: The collection consists of interviews and footage of events documenting lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) life in Eastern and Central Europe from the 1992 to 2009. The collection also includes a small amount of printed materials from the countries documented.

langmaterial.language: The papers are in English, Croatian, Czech, French, German, Hungarian, Polish, and Russian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of the creator, copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Douglas Conrad Eastern and Central European LGBT Interviews and Supporting Materials (MS 1907). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Descriptions of the interviews and biographical notes on the interviewees were provided by the creator.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Douglas Conrad was born July 25, 1958, in Omaha, Nebraska. Since the 1980s, he has served on several lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) and film arts organizations in the United States and Europe. Most notably, he helped coordinate several LGBT pride events and LGBT film festivals including many in Budapest. He has chronicled LGBT lives in Central and Eastern Europe since 1988. In 2007, he made Belgrade Pride , a short film about Belgrade's first LGBT pride celebration in 2001.

He attributes his interest in LGBT people in Eastern and Central Europe to several events. In 1987 he served on the Northern California and San Francisco/Bay Area Steering Committees for the March on Washington for Lesbian and Gay Rights. Its slogan, "we are everywhere," sparked his interest in the lives of LGBT people from other countries. His work for the 1988 San Francisco Freedom Day Parade and Celebration put him in contact with LGBT people from over 40 different countries, including Ilse Kornreich, founder of Argentina's first lesbian organization. Kornreich told Conrad of a gay man who was kicked out of East Germany into West Germany for erecting a pink triangle in honor of holocaust victims. Though unsuccessful in finding the man, Conrad met other LGBT people whose stories he captured in a video archive he titled Shades of Red & Pink .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of interviews and footage of events documenting lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) life in Eastern and Central Europe from the 1992 to 2009. Interviews from the Shades of Red & Pink archives, Douglas' collective title for the project, and a small amount of printed material from the countries documented are included. Germany, Hungary, and Russia are particularly well documented.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are arranged in two sections: Interviews and Location Footage and Supporting Materials, and alphabetically by country therein.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Milli is a gay man in his eighties. Sasa is in his twenties.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Radi? is an openly gay man in Croatia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Spehar is an openly lesbian woman in Croatia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ji?í was the president of the first gay organization in the Czech Republic and editor of the first gay magazine, SOHO . He had been a well known TV actor.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hyankova was a writer for SOHO .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape has shots of the sunset over the socialist housing, of the main square at night, of the view from the bridge and castle at night, of Letenske Sady Park, and of Wenzelplatz at Vallvske Namesti.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jan is the uncle of a Czech woman living in the United States. He and his lover were reported to the Czech authorities and a "neighborhood watch person" was appointed to live in his house by the communist government.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jan and Fritzek are a gay couple in their eighties. One was a pharmacist, and the other was a dancer in the Czech national ballet.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tasinato is a gay man in his sixties who lived as an openly gay man and drag queen in Czechoslovakia.

did.unittitle: England, Shots of Karl Marx's grave

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape includes some Klara Zetkin material as well as miscellaneous footage.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: In 1993, activists were found murdered in Mexico. Participants at the ILGA conference organized demonstrations at the Mexican Embassy in Paris, and the police violently assaulted the demonstrators.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hyankova was a writer for SOHO , the first gay magazine in the Czech Republic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape contains miscellanious footage and an interview with Karla Hyankova.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Beerlage is a West German scholar and activist, and the founder of SO 36. She is featured in Barbara Hammer's Nitrate Kisses .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The interview is about early homosexual rights and the socialist movement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape contains shots of the Rosa Luxemburg Monument, the Zionist Church, and the Magnus Hirschfield Monument.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: De La Motte-Sherman is the founder and editor of the first Eat German gay magazine, Die Anderer Welt .

did.unittitle: Election Debate for German Parliament about Homosexuality

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The debate includes Christina Schenk and other candidates.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Guenter is an Eastern German gay scholar in his fifties.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kotzendorfer is an East German lesbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Krug is an East German lesbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Father Loske was a gay priest and pastor of St. Peter's Shiftskirche.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The locations were filmed with Colin De La Motte-Sherman. They include the bathroom at Alexander place and Alt Berliner Bierstube. The footage also includes conversations with De La Motte-Sherman and bartenders and patrons from the Schonhauser Avlee Sonntag club.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Opa is a gay man who was in a concentration camp in Nazi Gemany.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Schadow, who is in his fifties, is a gay man living with his wife, lover, and son.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Schenck was the first openly lesbian member of the East German Parliament.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Schulz was an East German punk rock lesbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sillge was the founder of East German Lesbian Archive.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Von Mahlsdorf lives as a transexual in East Germany.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Borrows, who was an English woman, moved to Hungary in the early 1980s. She was the founder of the University Gender Studies.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gabor was the founder and editor of the Hungary Gay Magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The interview is about Gabor's personal history.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kis is a lesbian in her forties. She was a stone mason. She is married and has two children.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Laszlo is the founder and editor of the Hungary Gay Magazine.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Long is an American who lived in Hungary and Romania.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Szineg was the founder of Hungary's first hotline for domestic violence.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mosconaki was the founder of Gay Hotline and Háttér, the largest LGBT organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sandor discusses the Fullbright gender studies program in New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Gda?sk is the city where the Solidarity Movement began.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Initiative Gda?sk was the first LGBT organization in Gda?sk that focused on HIV education.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an interview with Initiative Gda?sk and Atinaczet , Poland's first gay magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple intellectual works on a single videocassette. This content begins 18 minutes, 30 seconds into the recording.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See: Last night at Kevin's, Circa 1994

langmaterial.language: The interview is in Polish and English.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jaromir is an openly gay man in his sixties. He is a Polish prince and a puppeteer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an interview with Jaromir and his lover Tadeusz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an interview with Jaromir and his lover Tadeusz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape cosists of an interview with Jaromir and Zbigniew, who was Jaromir's lover as well as a poet and a song writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape cosists of an interview with Jaromir and Zbigniew, who was Jaromir's lover as well as a poet and a song writer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an account of Jaromir in theaters and other Warsaw locations.

langmaterial.language: The interviews are in German, Polish, and English.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Wolfgang is East German but he has lived in Poland for many years.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Grzegorz was the only openly gay writer for the Solidarity Movement and a friend of Alan Ginsberg.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Soft Press was the publisher for Inaczej.

did.unittitle: Shapiro, David (also known as Gabriel David Tudose)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Shapiro was an activist in the gay community.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: AESOPS was the Russian AIDS Foundation. Kevin Gardner, an american, was the director.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an account on various Moscow locations such as Lenin Hills, Bolshoi Ballet, and Red Square.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape gives an account of statues and monuments in Moscow.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Multiple intellectual works on a single videocassette. This content starts at the beginning of the recording and ends after 18 minutes, 30 seconds into the recording.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bikmnkametova is a Russian lesbian in her twenties.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bykow is a Russian gay man.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Goloburdo is a lesbian woman from Russia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kuzmin is a Russian gay man in his forties, who was the head of the HIV Organization.

langmaterial.language: The footage is in Russian and English.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Pink Flamingo Film Festival was the first and last gay and lesbian festival that took place in Siberia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Raspopov was a Russian gay man in his seventies who was imprisoned for being gay.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sivkov is a Russian gay man in his forties.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Sudenko is a Russian gay man.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tkachenko is a gay man in his forties.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The tape consists of an interview with various gay men and lesbians of Russia including Boris, Oleg, Dima, Tatyana and Asya.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mirkovi? is a Serbian lesbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mladenovi? was the first openly lesbian Serbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Todosijevi? is a Serbian lesbian.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Vujanic is gay man in his thirties who is HIV positive.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: ?korjanc, a writer, was one of the first openly gay men in Slovenia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Tratnik, a writer, was one of the first openly lesbian women in Slovenia.

did.unittitle: General, "Being Able to Say Neither/Or," by Cynthia Cockburn

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original born digital files, as well as preservation masters, may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist request that they be made. Born digital files cannot be accessed remotely. System requirements include a Manuscripts and Archives computer and file viewing software.
